task_description:
Screen each abstract for relevance to machine learning for clinical text processing and NLP in healthcare. Rate methodological quality.

Relevance: RELEVANT if the paper applies NLP or ML to clinical notes, EHR text, medical literature, or patient-generated health text to extract clinical information or support clinical decisions. NOT_RELEVANT if the paper uses clinical data but not text, or applies NLP to non-clinical domains.

Quality rubric:
  1 = No methodology described or purely conceptual.
  2 = Approach mentioned but no metrics or comparisons.
  3 = Metrics and dataset described; limited baseline comparison.
  4 = Full methodology, multiple baselines, held-out test, reproducibility details.

papers.title: Recurrent Neural Networks for Structured Event Prediction from ICU Time-Series
papers.abstract: We train LSTM and GRU models on 48-hour ICU vital sign sequences to predict structured clinical events including acute kidney injury and septic shock. AUROC 0.86 is achieved for AKI onset prediction. The model processes structured numeric time-series only; no clinical text or NLP is involved.
papers.topic_hint: LSTM, ICU time-series, AKI prediction, structured data
papers.methodology_hint: LSTM/GRU, vital signs, AUROC 0.86, AKI prediction — structured data not text
papers.claimed_contribution: LSTM/GRU for ICU event prediction AUROC 0.86, structured vitals only, no NLP.

papers.title: ClinicalBERT for Named Entity Recognition of Clinical Concepts in Discharge Notes
papers.abstract: Named entity recognition of clinical concepts supports downstream EHR analytics. We fine-tune ClinicalBERT on the i2b2 2010 NER benchmark for problems, treatments, and tests. F1 0.89 is achieved, outperforming BioBERT, SciBERT, and a BiLSTM-CRF baseline. We release fine-tuned model weights and evaluate generalisation to two external corpora.
papers.topic_hint: ClinicalBERT, NER, discharge notes, i2b2, clinical concepts
papers.methodology_hint: ClinicalBERT, i2b2 2010, F1 0.89, BioBERT/SciBERT/BiLSTM-CRF baselines, external generalisation
papers.claimed_contribution: ClinicalBERT NER F1 0.89 on i2b2, 3 baselines, external generalisation, model released.

papers.title: Large Language Models Can Assist with Clinical Documentation
papers.abstract: LLMs may improve clinical documentation quality and reduce physician burden. We believe GPT-4 could help with structured data entry, ICD coding, and note summarisation based on informal trials in our department. No systematic evaluation, dataset, or metrics are reported in this commentary.
papers.methodology_hint: commentary, no evaluation, no metrics, no dataset
papers.claimed_contribution: Commentary arguing LLMs could assist clinical documentation.

papers.title: Extracting Social Determinants of Health from Clinical Notes Using Transformer Models
papers.abstract: Social determinants of health (SDOH) are underreported in structured EHR data but often documented in clinical notes. We fine-tune RoBERTa on 6,000 annotated clinical notes to extract eight SDOH categories including housing insecurity, food access, and social isolation. Macro-F1 0.76 is achieved. We compare against logistic regression and ClinicalBERT baselines and provide annotation guidelines.
papers.topic_hint: RoBERTa, SDOH extraction, clinical notes, social determinants
papers.methodology_hint: RoBERTa, 6k annotated notes, macro-F1 0.76, LR and ClinicalBERT baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: RoBERTa for SDOH extraction from clinical notes, macro-F1 0.76, 2 baselines.

papers.title: Graph Neural Network for Drug Interaction Prediction from Chemical Structures
papers.abstract: We encode molecular graphs of drug pairs and apply a bilinear interaction GNN to predict drug-drug interaction severity. Trained on DrugBank and TWOSIDES, AUROC 0.91 is achieved. The model processes structural chemical representations, not clinical text. No NLP or EHR processing is involved.
papers.topic_hint: GNN, drug-drug interactions, molecular graphs, DrugBank, chemical structures
papers.methodology_hint: bilinear GNN, DrugBank/TWOSIDES, AUROC 0.91 — chemical structures not clinical text
papers.claimed_contribution: GNN for drug interaction prediction AUROC 0.91 from molecular graphs, no clinical NLP.

papers.title: Summarisation of Clinical Notes Using Sequence-to-Sequence Models
papers.abstract: Automatic summarisation of clinical notes can save physician time. We fine-tune a BART model on 2,400 physician-written summaries of inpatient notes paired with full encounter text. ROUGE-L 0.43 is achieved. Performance is measured against reference summaries but no comparison with baseline summarisation models (extractive or prior seq2seq) is provided.
papers.topic_hint: BART, clinical note summarisation, seq2seq, ROUGE
papers.methodology_hint: BART, 2400 note-summary pairs, ROUGE-L 0.43 — no baseline comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: BART for clinical note summarisation, ROUGE-L 0.43, no summarisation baselines.

papers.title: Automated ICD Coding from Discharge Summaries: A Benchmark Study
papers.abstract: We systematically benchmark eight automated ICD coding approaches on MIMIC-III discharge summaries covering over 8,000 unique codes. Methods include flat classifiers, hierarchical label attention, and graph-based code dependency models. Micro-F1 0.60 is achieved by the best model. We provide standardised data splits, training code, and evaluation scripts to enable direct comparison of future work.
papers.topic_hint: ICD coding, discharge summaries, MIMIC-III, benchmark, NLP
papers.methodology_hint: 8 methods benchmarked, MIMIC-III, micro-F1 0.60, standardised splits, public code
papers.claimed_contribution: Benchmark of 8 ICD coding methods on MIMIC-III, micro-F1 0.60, standardised evaluation.

papers.title: Patient Phenotyping from EHR Using Temporal Convolutional Networks on Diagnosis Codes
papers.abstract: We apply temporal convolutional networks to sequences of ICD-10 diagnosis codes from 240,000 patient EHR records to identify 20 clinical phenotypes including heart failure and COPD exacerbation. F1 0.81 is achieved averaged across phenotypes. The model processes sequences of coded diagnoses only; no free text processing occurs.
papers.topic_hint: TCN, EHR, phenotyping, ICD-10 codes, temporal sequences
papers.methodology_hint: TCN, 240k EHR records, ICD-10 codes, F1 0.81 — coded data not text NLP
papers.claimed_contribution: TCN for EHR phenotyping from ICD codes, F1 0.81, no free-text processing.

papers.title: Zero-Shot Clinical Relation Extraction with GPT-4 and Prompt Engineering
papers.abstract: Extracting clinical relations from notes is annotation-intensive. We evaluate GPT-4 in zero-shot and 5-shot settings on three clinical relation extraction benchmarks: i2b2 2010, SemEval 2010 Task 8 biomedical, and DDI Extraction 2013. Zero-shot F1 0.63 is achieved on i2b2, comparable to task-specific fine-tuned models but without labelled training data. We systematically vary prompt structure and report sensitivity.
papers.topic_hint: GPT-4, zero-shot, clinical relation extraction, i2b2, prompt engineering
papers.methodology_hint: GPT-4, zero-shot, 3 benchmarks, F1 0.63, prompt sensitivity analysis — no fine-tuning baseline
papers.claimed_contribution: GPT-4 zero-shot clinical relation extraction F1 0.63 on i2b2, prompt sensitivity study.

papers.title: Attention-Based Deep Learning for Sepsis Detection from Vital Sign Streams
papers.abstract: We develop a self-attention model for real-time sepsis onset detection from continuous vital sign streams, trained on 55,000 MIMIC-IV admissions. AUROC 0.88 and sensitivity 0.82 at specificity 0.86 are achieved. The model processes time-series data only; no clinical notes or textual features are used.
papers.topic_hint: attention model, sepsis detection, vital signs, MIMIC-IV, time-series
papers.methodology_hint: self-attention, MIMIC-IV, AUROC 0.88, vital signs only — no clinical text
papers.claimed_contribution: Self-attention sepsis detection AUROC 0.88 from vital streams, no NLP component.
